skill.name: rmcp-quickstart
skill.description: Guide developers through creating, testing, and extending Rust MCP servers with the rmcp crate.
skill.summary: Create a working Rust MCP server with rmcp, from setup through local testing.
skill.author: EmilLindfors

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, weak cryptography, network, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown examples, Rust attributes, normal cargo commands, or documentation links with no malicious intent or hidden execution. The skill is low risk because users may run development commands and visit external resources.

low_findings.title: Instructional Shell Commands Require Normal Caution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples for cargo test, cargo new, cd, and cargo run. They are legitimate development steps, but running cargo commands can execute local build scripts or tests in the user project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible instructional examples and not hidden execution. The residual risk is the normal risk of running cargo commands in a development workspace.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links Are Present
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links to rmcp, MCP, example servers, and Tokio documentation. No credential transfer, tracking endpoint, or automatic network request was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown URLs for developer references. There is no evidence that the skill sends data to those URLs.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, network reconnaissance, and fetch findings map to ordinary prose, Rust derives, tool descriptions, and a sample fetch function name. No cryptographic operation, system probing, or reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows benign Markdown and Rust tutorial code at the flagged locations. No evidence found for malicious cryptography, probing, or data collection.

content.user_title: Build Rust MCP Servers with rmcp
content.value_statement: Starting a Rust MCP server requires several concepts, crate choices, and testing steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured rmcp quickstart for building a working server.
actual_capabilities: Explains MCP tools, resources, prompts, and transport choices.
Shows how to add rmcp, tokio, serde, schemars, and thiserror dependencies.
Provides a simple GreetingService example with rmcp tool macros.
Guides local testing with cargo test and cargo run.
Covers common rmcp patterns for state, complex parameters, and errors.
Lists next steps for resources, prompts, transports, tests, and deployment.
limitations: It is a tutorial skill, not an rmcp code generator with repository access.
Version references may become outdated and should be verified before release work.
It focuses on server basics and does not cover advanced production security in depth.
It does not validate that generated Rust code compiles unless tools are available.

use_cases.title: Create a First MCP Server
use_cases.description: Use the guide to understand rmcp setup, tool macros, and a small working server.

use_cases.title: Prototype Local AI Tools
use_cases.description: Build local stdio tools that expose Rust functions to MCP-compatible assistants.

use_cases.title: Plan Server Structure
use_cases.description: Use the project layout and testing sections to organize tools, resources, prompts, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Explain rmcp Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain MCP and rmcp to me as a Rust developer. Focus on tools, resources, prompts, and transports.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Minimal Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a minimal rmcp server with one greeting tool and one arithmetic tool. Explain each file I need.

prompt_templates.title: Add Typed Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add an rmcp tool with structured parameters, validation, and clear error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Review an rmcp Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my rmcp server design for transport choice, testing strategy, error handling, and production readiness.

output_examples.input: I need a local MCP server in Rust for a greeting tool.
output_examples.output: The skill explains the stdio transport, the service struct, and the rmcp tool macro pattern. It then outlines the files and tests needed for a first server.

output_examples.input: My rmcp tool needs a search query, limit, and offset.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends a typed parameter structure with serde and schemars derives. It explains when to aggregate parameters for cleaner tool signatures.

output_examples.input: My tool is not visible to the assistant.
output_examples.output: The skill checks for tool descriptions, the tool box macro on the implementation block, async functions, and compatible return types.

best_practices: Start with one small stdio tool before adding resources or remote transports.
Write unit tests for tool logic and integration tests for server behavior.
Verify crate versions and rmcp API changes before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not expose broad filesystem or network access without clear user controls.
Do not skip tool descriptions, because assistants need clear capability metadata.
Do not deploy remote transports before adding authentication and operational safeguards.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you build a Rust MCP server with the rmcp crate and test it locally.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It contains tutorial commands such as cargo test and cargo run for the user to execute.

faq.question: Is this only for beginner Rust developers?
faq.answer: No. It starts with basics and also covers state, complex parameters, errors, and testing strategy.

faq.question: Can it help with remote MCP servers?
faq.answer: It introduces SSE and HTTP transports, but production deployment needs additional security planning.

faq.question: Should I verify the rmcp version?
faq.answer: Yes. Check the current crate documentation before starting a production project.
